prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is in black and white and appears to have an aged film look, with visible grain and frame-edge artifacts.

At the beginning, the image is very dark and indistinct. Only faint shapes are visible, including a small circular mark or shape near the upper right, but the content cannot be clearly identified.

The next visible scene shows a close-up of a lower leg and feet. A patterned trouser leg descends from the top of the frame. Two dark dress shoes are shown near the bottom, separated from each other, with the foot or ankle positioned between/above them. The scene appears to involve water or a reflective liquid surface, though the exact setting is unclear from the frame.

Later, the commercial cuts to a close-up of a wrist and hand against a plain light background. A watch or watchband is fastened around the wrist. Radiating graphic lines around the wrist suggest emphasis, shine, or attention being drawn to the watchband. A shadow of the hand and wrist is visible on the background.

The final frame shows a product or brand end card. A triangular emblem contains the large letters “JB.” Beneath it, bold text reads “WATCHBANDS.” The layout centers the logo and product category on a plain background.
